title: Periodicity of chemical properties of the elements in Period 3
summary: 9701 AS Period 3 oxide and chloride chemistry — acidic, basic, and amphoteric behaviour with PhET acid–base sim.
learningObjectives: Classify Period 3 oxides by acid–base character.
Predict products of oxide reactions with water.
Explain hydrolysis of chlorides and pH of solutions.
Link bonding type to chemical behaviour.

simpleExplanation.title: Oxides and chlorides across Period 3
simpleExplanation.summary: Oxides change from basic (Na, Mg) through amphoteric (Al) to acidic (Si, P, S, Cl). Chloride hydrolysis follows similar charge-density logic.
steps: Na₂O + H₂O → NaOH (pH > 7); SO₃ + H₂O → H₂SO₄ (pH < 7).
Al₂O₃ reacts with both acids and bases — amphoteric.
Higher oxidation state → more covalent, acidic oxide character.

sections.content: **Chemical periodicity** (9.2) is a favourite structured question — tabulate **oxide + water → pH** and explain with **bonding** and **oxidation state**.
